member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.DocxReader
member.summary: A tiny, dependency-free reader for the Office Open XML word-processing (.docx) format — the sibling of . It opens the ZIP package by hand with and parses word/document.xml (and, when needed, word/styles.xml) with a streaming — no third-party library, no reflection, AOT- and trim-safe, so it runs unchanged in the browser WebAssembly runtime.

remarks.para: The reader produces the immutable model the Word viewer consumes, in document (reading) order. It maps the common WordprocessingML body constructs:

item.description: Headings from <w:pStyle w:val="Heading1..6"> (or Title → level 1). Non-standard style ids are resolved through word/styles.xml via the style's <w:name> or <w:outlineLvl>.
Lists from <w:numPr>: a paragraph carrying a numbering reference becomes a list item. Numbered vs. bulleted is a best-effort guess (see below).
Tables <w:tbl>/<w:tr>/<w:tc>; the first row is treated as the header row.

remarks.para: Deferred / not interpreted (best-effort, degrade gracefully): images and drawings; footnotes/endnotes, comments, and fields; hyperlinks render as their plain text; list nesting is flattened to a single level; the numbered-vs-bulleted decision reads w:numId heuristically (a numId of 0/absent is treated as bulleted) rather than resolving word/numbering.xml; run-level color/size/underline beyond bold/italic is dropped; merged table cells are read as individual cells. Anything unrecognized degrades to a plain paragraph.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.DocxReader.Read(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Parses a .docx byte stream into a : the body blocks in document (reading) order.
member.param: The raw bytes of a .docx file.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.DocxReader.Read(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Parses a .docx stream into a . The stream is read as a ZIP package; the caller owns its lifetime.
member.param: A seekable stream positioned at the start of a .docx file.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer
member.summary: Tier 2 styled, read-only, multi-sheet spreadsheet viewer over a parsed . Sheet tabs form a WAI-ARIA role="tablist"; the active sheet renders as a virtualized role="grid" whose row window comes from the reused DxVirtualize<T> primitive. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-spreadsheet.css). Inherits its sheet/keyboard state from .
member.remarks: We deliberately do not use DxDataGrid<TRow> here. That grid's binding is source-generated for compile-time-known column types, but a spreadsheet's columns are a runtime-defined schema (ADR-0009). So we window the rows with the general-purpose DxVirtualize<T> primitive and render cells straight from the workbook's string grid. The grid declares aria-rowcount/ aria-colcount for the full sheet so assistive technology reports the true size even though only the scroll window is in the DOM (WCAG 1.3.1).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.Class
member.summary: Optional extra CSS class on the viewer root.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.RowHeight
member.summary: Pixel height of each data row; drives the virtualization math.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxSpreadsheetViewer.ViewportHeight
member.summary: Pixel height of the scrollable grid viewport.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer
member.summary:
Tier 2 styled, read-only word-processing document viewer over a parsed . Renders each block as a real semantic element —

–

,

, / ,

    /
      /
    1. , and a with
      / — straight to the Blazor render tree. Styling is CSS-variable driven (see dx-word.css).

remarks.para: The rendering rule is the whole accessibility point of this component: every block becomes a true semantic element and all text flows through (HTML-encoded). There is no MarkupString and no hand-built HTML string anywhere — output is therefore both XSS-safe and the most accessible form for assistive technology: a genuine heading outline (WCAG 1.3.1 / 2.4.6), lists exposed as lists, table headers carrying scope, and DOM order equal to the document's reading order (WCAG 1.3.2).

Unlike , the document is rendered in full rather than virtualized: windowing top-level blocks would put a scroll container between the document landmark and its headings/lists/tables, breaking the heading outline and reading order that are this viewer's reason to exist. Correctness over windowing for v1 (the prompt sanctions this). There is likewise no separate Tier 1 primitive — the content is static, so there is no headless state worth splitting out; this single styled component is the whole surface.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer.Document
member.summary: The parsed document to display. Blocks render in document order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer.Class
member.summary: Optional extra CSS class on the viewer root.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.DxWordViewer.Label
member.summary: Accessible name for the document region (the aria-label on the focusable role="document" container). Defaults to "Document".

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.Workbook
member.summary: A parsed workbook: the ordered sheets read from an .xlsx file. This is the reader's output and the spreadsheet viewer's input — a plain, immutable data model with no reflection and no live file handle.
member.param: The worksheets, in workbook order.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.Workbook.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.Worksheet})
member.summary: A parsed workbook: the ordered sheets read from an .xlsx file. This is the reader's output and the spreadsheet viewer's input — a plain, immutable data model with no reflection and no live file handle.
member.param: The worksheets, in workbook order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.Workbook.Sheets
member.summary: The worksheets, in workbook order.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.Worksheet
member.summary: One worksheet: a name plus a dense grid of string cell values. The first row is treated as the header by the viewer. Ragged rows are padded to .
member.param: The sheet's display name (the tab label).
Rows of cell text, top to bottom; each row has cells.
The widest row's cell count (columns are uniform across the sheet).

member.summary: One worksheet: a name plus a dense grid of string cell values. The first row is treated as the header by the viewer. Ragged rows are padded to .
member.param: The sheet's display name (the tab label).
Rows of cell text, top to bottom; each row has cells.
The widest row's cell count (columns are uniform across the sheet).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.Worksheet.Name
member.summary: The sheet's display name (the tab label).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.Worksheet.Rows
member.summary: Rows of cell text, top to bottom; each row has cells.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.Worksheet.ColumnCount
member.summary: The widest row's cell count (columns are uniform across the sheet).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive
member.summary: Tier 1 headless state for a read-only spreadsheet viewer: which sheet is active, how to switch sheets, and the keyboard model for the sheet tab strip. It renders no markup — is the styled Tier 2 layer on top. Mirrors the self-contained view-switch pattern used by the scheduler.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.Workbook
member.summary: The workbook to display. Sheets render in workbook order as tabs.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveSheetIndex
member.summary: The zero-based index of the initially active sheet.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveSheetIndexChanged
member.summary: Raised when the active sheet changes (tab click or arrow keys).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.Sheets
member.summary: The sheets in workbook order (never null; empty when no workbook).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveSheet
member.summary: The currently active sheet, or null when the workbook has no sheets.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.ActiveIndex
member.summary: The clamped index of the active sheet.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.IsActive(System.Int32)
member.summary: Whether is the active sheet.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.SetActiveAsync(System.Int32)
member.summary: Activates the sheet at (no-op if unchanged/out of range).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.SpreadsheetViewerPrimitive.OnTabStripKeyDownAsync(Microsoft.AspNetCore.Components.Web.KeyboardEventArgs)
member.summary: Arrow/Home/End navigation across the tab strip (automatic activation).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordDocument
member.summary: A parsed word-processing document: the ordered top-level blocks read from a .docx file. This is 's output and DxWordViewer's input — a plain, immutable semantic model with no reflection and no live file handle. The block order is the document's reading order (WCAG 1.3.2).
member.param: The body blocks, top to bottom, in document order.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordDocument.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.WordBlock})
member.summary: A parsed word-processing document: the ordered top-level blocks read from a .docx file. This is 's output and DxWordViewer's input — a plain, immutable semantic model with no reflection and no live file handle. The block order is the document's reading order (WCAG 1.3.2).
member.param: The body blocks, top to bottom, in document order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordDocument.Blocks
member.summary: The body blocks, top to bottom, in document order.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordBlock
member.summary: One top-level block of a . The hierarchy is closed and pattern-matched by the viewer (no reflection): , , , and . Unknown constructs degrade to a of their plain text.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordHeading
member.summary: A heading paragraph (mapped from a Heading1–Heading6 or Title paragraph style). is clamped to 1–6 and drives the rendered <h1>–<h6> element so the document has a real, ordered heading outline (WCAG 1.3.1 / 2.4.6). A Title maps to level 1.
member.param: The heading level, 1 (most prominent) through 6.
The inline runs making up the heading text.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordHeading.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun})
member.summary: A heading paragraph (mapped from a Heading1–Heading6 or Title paragraph style). is clamped to 1–6 and drives the rendered <h1>–<h6> element so the document has a real, ordered heading outline (WCAG 1.3.1 / 2.4.6). A Title maps to level 1.
member.param: The heading level, 1 (most prominent) through 6.
The inline runs making up the heading text.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordHeading.Level
member.summary: The heading level, 1 (most prominent) through 6.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordHeading.Runs
member.summary: The inline runs making up the heading text.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordParagraph
member.summary:
A body paragraph: a sequence of inline s rendered as a

. An empty run list is a blank paragraph.
member.param: The inline runs making up the paragraph text, in order.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordParagraph.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun})
member.summary:
A body paragraph: a sequence of inline s rendered as a

. An empty run list is a blank paragraph.
member.param: The inline runs making up the paragraph text, in order.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordParagraph.Runs
member.summary: The inline runs making up the paragraph text, in order.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordList
member.summary: A list: either bulleted (<ul>) or numbered (<ol>), with each item a sequence of inline runs. Nesting is flattened in v1 — every item is a top-level <li> (see remarks).
member.param: for a numbered list; otherwise bulleted.
The list items, in order; each is its own run sequence.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordList.#ctor(System.Boolean,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun}})
member.summary: A list: either bulleted (<ul>) or numbered (<ol>), with each item a sequence of inline runs. Nesting is flattened in v1 — every item is a top-level <li> (see remarks).
member.param: for a numbered list; otherwise bulleted.
The list items, in order; each is its own run sequence.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordList.Ordered
member.summary: for a numbered list; otherwise bulleted.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordList.Items
member.summary: The list items, in order; each is its own run sequence.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordTable
member.summary: A table of rows of cells. The first row is treated as a header row: its cells render as <th scope="col">, the rest as <td> (WCAG 1.3.1). Each cell carries its own inline runs.
member.param: The rows, top to bottom; the first is the header row.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordTable.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.WordTableRow})
member.summary: A table of rows of cells. The first row is treated as a header row: its cells render as <th scope="col">, the rest as <td> (WCAG 1.3.1). Each cell carries its own inline runs.
member.param: The rows, top to bottom; the first is the header row.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordTable.Rows
member.summary: The rows, top to bottom; the first is the header row.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordTableRow
member.summary: One table row: its ordered cells.
member.param: The cells, left to right.

member.summary: The cells, left to right.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordTableCell.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun})
member.summary: One table cell: the inline runs of its (first) paragraph's text.
member.param: The inline runs making up the cell text.

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordTableCell.Runs
member.summary: The inline runs making up the cell text.

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun
member.summary: An inline run of text carrying best-effort character formatting. A run with no emphasis ( and both ) renders as bare text — the viewer never fakes a semantic element for an unstyled run.
member.param: The run's literal text.
Whether the run is bold (<w:b>).
Whether the run is italic (<w:i>).

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Boolean)
member.summary: An inline run of text carrying best-effort character formatting. A run with no emphasis ( and both ) renders as bare text — the viewer never fakes a semantic element for an unstyled run.
member.param: The run's literal text.
Whether the run is bold (<w:b>).
Whether the run is italic (<w:i>).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun.Text

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun.Bold
member.summary: Whether the run is bold (<w:b>).

member.name: P:BlazorDX.Documents.WordRun.Italic
member.summary: Whether the run is italic (<w:i>).

member.name: T:BlazorDX.Documents.XlsxReader
member.summary: A tiny, dependency-free reader for the Office Open XML spreadsheet (.xlsx) format. It is the inverse of BlazorDX.Primitives.Grid.XlsxWriter: it opens the ZIP package by hand with and parses the OOXML parts with a streaming — no third-party library, no reflection, AOT- and trim-safe, so it runs unchanged in the browser WebAssembly runtime.

remarks.para: The reader produces the same / model the spreadsheet viewer consumes. It round-trips what the writer produces and also handles the parts a real-world workbook adds: a shared-string table, t="str" formula-result strings, and ragged rows.

Deferred (Phase 3+): formulas surface their last cached <v> value, they are not recomputed; number formats, styles, and merged cells are not yet interpreted (cells carry their raw text). Dates therefore appear as their underlying serial number.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.XlsxReader.Read(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Parses an .xlsx byte stream into a : the sheets in workbook order, each as a dense, padded grid of string cell values.
member.param: The raw bytes of an .xlsx file.

member.name: M:BlazorDX.Documents.XlsxReader.Read(System.IO.Stream)
member.summary: Parses an .xlsx stream into a . The stream is read as a ZIP package; the caller owns its lifetime.
member.param: A seekable stream positioned at the start of an .xlsx file.
